content: {"perspective": "Economic", "answer": "The search results recover ONE published IF-specific economic evaluation plus several adjacent references. Sources 1, 3, and 5 (PMC12599020, ResearchGate, PubMed 41214519) all describe the same study: a trial-based analysis and lifetime model-based analysis of the cost-effectiveness of a one-year monthly 5-consecutive-day fasting-mimicking diet (FMD) programme in patients with type 2 diabetes. Source 2 (ScienceDirect) confirms this is described as 'the first [study] to evaluate the costs and consequences of adding the FMD fasting nutri-technology to diabetes standard care' — meaning peer-reviewed IF economic evaluation is essentially limited to ONE program (Longo FMD) in ONE population (T2D, not MetS). No ICER, QALY, or budget-impact values are disclosed in the snippets. The ICER institute type-2-diabetes report (Sources 4, 9, 10) uses a $100,000 and $150,000 per QALY / evLY threshold and the UKPDS-OM2 risk equations for T2D complications and mortality modeling — but evaluates tirzepatide and other medications, not IF. The ICER obesity medications report (Source 7) evaluates pharmacotherapy, not lifestyle/IF. Source 11 (PMC5695352 systematic review of cost-effectiveness of preventing type 2 diabetes) concludes 'Lifestyle programmes and metformin appeared to be cost effective in preventing diabetes in high-risk individuals (median incremental cost-effectiveness ratios...)' — establishing the comparator: generic lifestyle interventions are cost-effective, but IF is not called out separately. The NICE ph38 guidance on type 2 diabetes prevention (Source 13) provides the HE framework for lifestyle modelling. Sources 12 (TRE in T2D) and 15 (TRE 1-4% weight loss) and 14 (eTRE trial) offer clinical effect sizes but no economic evaluation. Information gap: NO search result provides an ICER per QALY gained, budget impact, or cost-offset figure for intermittent fasting specifically in metabolic syndrome. The ONE study recovered uses FMD in T2D, not TRE/ADF/5:2 in MetS. The economic evidence base for IF in MetS is essentially absent — a critical finding for payers and HTA bodies considering whether to support IF interventions.", "key_findings": ["Peer-reviewed IF economic evaluation is essentially ONE study (Sources 1, 2, 3, 5): a trial-based and lifetime model-based cost-utility analysis of a monthly 5-day fasting-mimicking diet (FMD) program in type 2 diabetes. Described as 'the first' such study.", "No ICER per QALY, budget impact, or cost-offset value is disclosed in the search snippets for the FMD study or for any IF intervention in metabolic syndrome specifically.", "The ICER institute type 2 diabetes report (Sources 4, 9, 10) establishes the methodological benchmark — UKPDS-OM2 risk equations, $100,000 / $150,000 per QALY thresholds — but evaluates tirzepatide and other medications, NOT intermittent fasting.", "The PMC5695352 systematic review (Source 11) finds lifestyle programmes and metformin are cost-effective for diabetes prevention in high-risk adults, but does not break out IF separately from generic lifestyle counseling.", "Critical gap: the economic evidence base for IF (TRE, ADF, 5:2, ICR) in metabolic syndrome is essentially absent from peer-reviewed HE literature. Payers and HTA bodies have no IF-specific ICER or budget-impact model to rely on — this is itself a major finding."], "sources_used": ["https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12599020/", "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1098301524028468", "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/397459835_Cost-effectiveness_of_a_periodic_fasting-mimicking_diet_programme_in_patients_with_type_2_diabetes_a_trial-based_analysis_and_a_lifetime_model-based_analysis", "https://icer.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/ICER_-Type-2-Diabetes_Public-Comment-Grid_01062022.pdf", "https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41214519/", "https://icer.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/ICER_Obesity_Final_Evidence_Report_and_Meeting_Summary_102022.pdf", "https://icer.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/Type-2-Diabetes-RAAG_FEB-2022.pdf", "https://icer.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/ICER_Obesity_Public-Comment-Folio_083122.pdf", "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5695352/", "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S026156142500216X", "https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ph38/evidence/he1-costeffectiveness-review-and-economic-modelling-435125485", "https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34418079/"]}
